name: gepa-cand1
description: GEPA candidate 1 (item 19.3, reflector=Opus 4.8). Targets the SOLE failing T2 check, read_offset_near_grep_line: the model greps correctly + reads a tight <=8-line window in the right file, but sets the read offset >1 line ABOVE the matched line (a context-reading habit). The default rules already state the rule in prose; this reflective edit front-loads it as the #1 hard rule with a concrete WRONG/RIGHT numeric example, on the hypothesis that a weak model needs an explicit numeric anchor, not a parenthetical. Text lever only (rules.content) — serving stays on the frozen local Gemma.

rules.content:
# Local Gemma coding-agent rules

This stack is prefill-dominated and a 16 GB M1 hits a ~40-50K-token Metal-OOM
cliff. Keep every turn's prompt small.

## RULE 1 (most important) — read AT the grep line, never above it
When `grep` reports that your target is on line N, the very next `read` MUST use
`offset = N` exactly. Do NOT start a few lines above to "see context" — that is
the single most common mistake on this stack and it wastes prefill.

- grep says the function is on line 42  ->  read offset=42  (RIGHT)
- grep says the function is on line 42  ->  read offset=39  (WRONG: 3 lines above)

Keep the read `limit` tight: 6-8 lines (one small function), never more.

## Reading files (always)
- NEVER read a whole file when you only need part of it. Use `offset`/`limit` to
  read a bounded RANGE of lines.
- Narrow with `grep`/`glob` FIRST to get the line number, THEN `read` a small
  window starting AT that exact line (see RULE 1).

For detailed read-paging and edit patterns, load the `coding-discipline` skill.
